perturbation.original_text: This document and appendices contain the entire agreement between the Parties and supersede any previous oral or written understandings, commitments or agreements pertaining to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This document and appendices contain the entire agreement between the Parties and supersede any previous oral or written understandings, commitments or agreements pertaining to the subject matter hereof, except for any pre-existing agreements specifically acknowledged in writing by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except for any pre-existing agreements specifically acknowledged in writing by both parties', the clause now permits certain prior agreements to remain in effect, thus contradicting the initial claim that *this* document is the *entire* agreement. This undermines the principle of integration and introduces ambiguity about which agreements govern the parties' relationship. The document is no longer containing the entire agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This document and appendices contain the entire agreement between the Parties and supersede any previous oral or written understandings, commitments or agreements pertaining to the subject matter hereof, except for any pre-existing agreements specifically acknowledged in writing by both parties. This MNDA shall not be modified or changed in any manner except in a writing signed by both parties. If a court of competent jurisdiction finds any of the provisions hereto so over-broad as to be unenforceable, such provisions may be reduced in scope by the court to the extent it deems necessary to render the provision reasonable and enforceable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the original clause stating that the document contains the 'entire agreement'. The addition of 'except for any pre-existing agreements specifically acknowledged in writing by both parties' directly contradicts the initial statement. The contradicted text is essentially the same as the Changed Text, reinforcing the fact that the clause itself now contains an in-text contradiction, because it asserts that the document is the entire agreement while simultaneously allowing for other agreements to exist. This aligns with the Ambiguities-In Text Contradiction definition provided. By adding an exception, it undermines the original intention, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This MNDA shall not be modified or changed in any manner except in a writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This MNDA shall not be modified or changed in any manner except in a writing signed by both parties, or through documented email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'or through documented email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties' creates an alternative method to modify the agreement, contrasting the original statement about written and signed modifications. This creates conflicting rules regarding modifications. It is not clear on whether email correspondence are official modifications, because the first part states that they aren't.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This document and appendices contain the entire agreement between the Parties and supersede any previous oral or written understandings, commitments or agreements pertaining to the subject matter hereof, except for any pre-existing agreements specifically acknowledged in writing by both parties. This MNDA shall not be modified or changed in any manner except in a writing signed by both parties, or through documented email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties. If a court of competent jurisdiction finds any of the provisions hereto so over-broad as to be unenforceable, such provisions may be reduced in scope by the court to the extent it deems necessary to render the provision reasonable and enforceable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an alternative method (email correspondence) for modifying the MNDA, which directly contradicts the original text stating that modifications can only be made in writing signed by both parties. This creates ambiguity and a clear in-text contradiction because the contradicted text contains both the original and changed text. The original text stated that the MNDA can only be changed in a writing signed by both parties. The changed text allows the MNDA to be changed in writing signed by both parties OR through documented email correspondence between authorized representatives of both parties. This creates an in-text contradiction.
